basic_test_from_file.description: Basic test loading app from file
basic_test_from_file.code:
import streamlit as st
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest

def test_app():
    """Test the app runs without errors."""
    at = AppTest.from_file("app.py")
    at.run()
    assert not at.exception
basic_test_from_file.use_case: Quick smoke test to verify app runs

basic_test_from_string.code:
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest

def test_simple_app():
    """Test app defined as string."""
    code = """
import streamlit as st
st.title("Hello World")
"""
    at = AppTest.from_string(code)
    at.run()
    assert not at.exception
    assert at.title[0].value == "Hello World"
basic_test_from_string.use_case: Test small code snippets or generated code

basic_test_from_function.code:
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest
import streamlit as st

def my_app():
    st.title("My App")
    name = st.text_input("Name")
    if name:
        st.write(f"Hello, {name}!")

def test_my_app():
    """Test app defined as function."""
    at = AppTest.from_function(my_app)
    at.run()
    assert not at.exception
    assert at.title[0].value == "My App"

test_button_click.code:
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest

def test_button_interaction():
    """Test button click and state change."""
    at = AppTest.from_file("app.py")
    at.run()
    
    # Initial state
    assert "Button clicked" not in at.markdown[0].value
    
    # Click button
    at.button[0].click().run()
    
    # Verify state changed
    assert "Button clicked" in at.markdown[0].value
test_button_click.use_case: Test button click events and side effects

test_text_input.code:
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest

def test_text_input_widget():
    """Test text input and response."""
    at = AppTest.from_file("app.py")
    at.run()
    
    # Enter text
    at.text_input[0].input("Alice").run()
    
    # Verify output
    assert "Hello, Alice!" in at.markdown[0].value
    
    # Change text
    at.text_input[0].input("Bob").run()
    assert "Hello, Bob!" in at.markdown[0].value
test_text_input.use_case: Test text input and dynamic content

test_checkbox.code:
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest

def test_checkbox_toggle():
    """Test checkbox state changes."""
    at = AppTest.from_file("app.py")
    at.run()
    
    # Check initial state
    assert not at.checkbox[0].value
    
    # Toggle checkbox
    at.checkbox[0].check().run()
    assert at.checkbox[0].value
    
    # Uncheck
    at.checkbox[0].uncheck().run()
    assert not at.checkbox[0].value
test_checkbox.use_case: Test checkbox interactions and conditional logic

test_selectbox.code:
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest

def test_selectbox_selection():
    """Test selectbox option selection."""
    at = AppTest.from_file("app.py")
    at.run()
    
    # Select by value
    at.selectbox[0].select("Option 2").run()
    assert at.selectbox[0].value == "Option 2"
    
    # Select by index
    at.selectbox[0].select_index(0).run()
    assert at.selectbox[0].value == "Option 1"

test_slider.code:
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest

def test_slider_values():
    """Test slider value changes."""
    at = AppTest.from_file("app.py")
    at.run()
    
    # Set slider value
    at.slider[0].set_value(50).run()
    assert at.slider[0].value == 50
    
    # Verify computed output
    assert "Value: 50" in at.text[0].value

test_file_uploader.code:
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest
import io

def test_file_upload():
    """Test file upload functionality."""
    at = AppTest.from_file("app.py")
    at.run()
    
    # Create mock CSV file
    csv_content = b"name,age\nAlice,30\nBob,25"
    file = io.BytesIO(csv_content)
    file.name = "test.csv"
    
    # Upload file
    at.file_uploader[0].upload(file).run()
    
    # Verify processing
    assert "2 rows" in at.text[0].value

test_session_state.code:
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest

def test_session_state_management():
    """Test session state read and write."""
    at = AppTest.from_file("app.py")
    
    # Set session state before running
    at.session_state["counter"] = 5
    at.run()
    
    # Verify state used in app
    assert at.markdown[0].value == "Count: 5"
    
    # Click increment button
    at.button[0].click().run()
    
    # Verify state updated
    assert at.session_state["counter"] == 6

test_secrets.code:
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest

def test_with_secrets():
    """Test app with secrets."""
    at = AppTest.from_file("app.py")
    
    # Set secrets before running
    at.secrets["api_key"] = "test-key-123"
    at.secrets["db_password"] = "secret-pass"
    at.run()
    
    # Verify app uses secrets correctly
    assert not at.exception
    assert "Connected" in at.success[0].value

test_form_submission.code:
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest

def test_form_complete_flow():
    """Test form input and submission."""
    at = AppTest.from_file("app.py")
    at.run()
    
    # Fill form fields
    at.text_input[0].input("John Doe").run()
    at.number_input[0].set_value(30).run()
    at.selectbox[0].select("Engineer").run()
    
    # Submit form
    at.button[0].click().run()
    
    # Verify submission processed
    assert "Form submitted" in at.success[0].value
    assert "John Doe" in at.markdown[0].value

test_with_fixture.description: Use pytest fixture for test setup
test_with_fixture.code:
import pytest
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest

@pytest.fixture
def app_test():
    """Fixture to create AppTest instance."""
    at = AppTest.from_file("app.py")
    at.run()
    return at

def test_initial_state(app_test):
    """Test initial app state."""
    assert not app_test.exception
    assert app_test.title[0].value == "My App"

def test_user_interaction(app_test):
    """Test user interaction."""
    app_test.button[0].click().run()
    assert "Clicked" in app_test.text[0].value
test_with_fixture.use_case: Reuse setup code across multiple tests

test_parametrized.code:
import pytest
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest

@pytest.mark.parametrize("input_value,expected", [
    ("Alice", "Hello, Alice!"),
    ("Bob", "Hello, Bob!"),
    ("", "Please enter a name"),
])
def test_greeting_variations(input_value, expected):
    """Test app with different inputs."""
    at = AppTest.from_file("app.py")
    at.run()
    
    if input_value:
        at.text_input[0].input(input_value).run()
    
    assert expected in at.markdown[0].value

test_error_handling.code:
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest
import pytest

def test_invalid_input_handling():
    """Test app handles invalid input gracefully."""
    at = AppTest.from_file("app.py")
    at.run()
    
    # Enter invalid input
    at.number_input[0].set_value(-1).run()
    
    # Verify error shown
    assert at.error
    assert "must be positive" in at.error[0].value

def test_app_with_expected_error():
    """Test app that should raise exception."""
    code = """
import streamlit as st
raise ValueError("Expected error")
"""
    at = AppTest.from_string(code)
    
    with pytest.raises(Exception):
        at.run()
test_error_handling.use_case: Test error messages and exception handling

test_multipage_navigation.code:
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest

def test_page_navigation():
    """Test navigation between pages."""
    at = AppTest.from_file("app.py")
    at.run()
    
    # Verify initial page
    assert "Home Page" in at.title[0].value
    
    # Navigate to different page
    # Note: Use button/selectbox that triggers navigation
    at.button[1].click().run()
    
    # Verify page changed
    assert "Analysis Page" in at.title[0].value
test_multipage_navigation.use_case: Test page routing and state across pages

test_data_visualization.code:
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest
import pandas as pd

def test_chart_creation():
    """Test that charts are created correctly."""
    at = AppTest.from_file("app.py")
    at.run()
    
    # Verify chart exists
    assert len(at.line_chart) > 0
    
    # Verify chart data
    chart_data = at.line_chart[0].data
    assert isinstance(chart_data, pd.DataFrame)
    assert len(chart_data) > 0

test_with_mock.code:
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock

@patch('app.fetch_data_from_api')
def test_with_mocked_api(mock_fetch):
    """Test app with mocked API call."""
    # Setup mock
    mock_fetch.return_value = pd.DataFrame({
        'name': ['Alice', 'Bob'],
        'value': [10, 20]
    })
    
    at = AppTest.from_file("app.py")
    at.run()
    
    # Verify mock was called
    mock_fetch.assert_called_once()
    
    # Verify app processed mocked data
    assert "Alice" in at.dataframe[0].value.to_string()
    assert "Bob" in at.dataframe[0].value.to_string()

test_cached_function.code:
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest
import streamlit as st

def test_caching_behavior():
    """Test that caching works correctly."""
    at = AppTest.from_file("app.py")
    
    # First run - data loaded
    at.run()
    assert not at.exception
    initial_data = at.dataframe[0].value
    
    # Second run - should use cache
    at.run()
    assert not at.exception
    cached_data = at.dataframe[0].value
    
    # Verify data is identical (from cache)
    assert initial_data.equals(cached_data)

test_suite_template.code:
"""Test suite for Streamlit app."""
import pytest
from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest
from pathlib import Path

# Path to app
APP_PATH = Path(__file__).parent / "app.py"

@pytest.fixture
def app():
    """Create AppTest instance."""
    at = AppTest.from_file(str(APP_PATH))
    at.run()
    return at

class TestBasicFunctionality:
    """Test basic app functionality."""
    
    def test_app_loads(self, app):
        """Test app loads without errors."""
        assert not app.exception
    
    def test_title_present(self, app):
        """Test title is displayed."""
        assert len(app.title) > 0

class TestUserInteractions:
    """Test user interactions."""
    
    def test_button_click(self, app):
        """Test button functionality."""
        app.button[0].click().run()
        assert not app.exception
    
    def test_input_processing(self, app):
        """Test input processing."""
        app.text_input[0].input("test").run()
        assert "test" in app.markdown[0].value

if __name__ == "__main__":
    pytest.main([__file__, "-v"])
test_suite_template.use_case: Starting point for comprehensive test suite
